Manufacturing and trading of veterinary vaccines and health products
Manufacturing and trading of veterinary vaccines and health products
Manufacture of pharmaceuticals, medicinal chemical and botanical products
Wholesale of pharmaceutical and medical goods

Hester is the second largest poultry vaccine manufacturer in India. Its customer base in India includes stockists, retailers, dairy farms, pharmaceutical companies, veterinary professionals, government bodies, and farmer community. The Company also provides vaccines to other countries government and global bodies like FAO, UN, etc. as part of global PPR and other vaccination initiatives. Additionally, as part of its pet care range, the Company also services household customers pan-India.

Hester Biosciences Tanzania
Limited (Wholly-owned subsidiary of the Hester Biosciences Kenya Limited)
The use of materials and processes that tend to produce greenhouse gas emissions significantly increases the environmental risks associated with GHG emissions.

a) Implemented solar power generation at manufacturing sites to renewable energy use.
b) Enhance energy and operational efficiency of equipment to minimize overall energy consumption.

Improper waste management poses significant risks to businesses due to the potential hazards it may present to relevant stakeholders, including employees, communities, and regulators. Additionally, the continuous increase in waste generation intensifies environmental impacts and presents growing challenges for effective management.

a) Prioritize process optimization and material efficiency to minimize waste generation during operations.

b) Establish systems for proper segregation of waste to facilitate recycling and safe disposal, in compliance with local regulations.

c) Conduct regular training sessions to build awareness among staff on responsible waste handling and reduction practices.

d) Collaborate with certified waste management vendors and explore innovative technologies such as waste-to-energy or circular economy models.

e) Implement waste tracking systems to monitor quantities, types, and disposal methods, enabling data-driven decisions and transparency in sustainability reporting.

Water is a shared and limited resource, underscoring the need for responsible and efficient usage. Sustainable water consumption is critical to maintaining social and environmental responsibility. Furthermore, stringent regulations governing the reuse and recycling of treated water present compliance challenges and operational risks, reinforcing the need for robust water management practices.

a) Implemented cutting-edge technologies and optimized processes to significantly reduce our overall water consumption.
b) Investments in modern wastewater treatment infrastructure ensure our facilities meet and often exceed regulatory compliance for water discharge and reuse.
c) Established the ETP lab for continues and real-time monitoring to maintain strict adherence to water discharge and reuse standards. 
d) Adopting a ZLD strategy, we aim to recycle and reuse all treated water within our operations, substantially reducing the intake of fresh water.
e) Actively engage and educate both internal teams and external partners to promote water conservation awareness and responsible usage practices.
Attracting / retaining talent and workforce welfare

Risk: Animal healthcare companies face intense competition for skilled professionals. The industry depends on highly qualified individuals to drive product innovation, conduct clinical trials, navigate regulatory requirements and successfully commercialise new products. A shortage of talent or high employee turnover could impede progress in these critical areas.

Opportunity: The company’s proactive focus on workforce welfare and development demonstrates a strong commitment to nurturing its most valuable asset—its people. By investing in employee growth and well-being, the company can attract and retain top talent, fostering innovation and operational excellence.

Hester aims to retain its employees by implementing progressive, employee-friendly practices, particularly given the limited pool of available talent. This strategic approach provides a competitive advantage by fostering deep subject matter expertise. Additionally, investing in training programs to up skill existing employees helps build internal capacity to drive innovation and advance environmental goals.

Supply chain partners, operating across diverse locations, are responsible for the timely delivery of input materials, products, services, and capital goods essential to business operations. Ensuring the quality and punctuality of these inputs is critical for uninterrupted operations and business continuity. Any disruption or threat to the sustainability of these supply chain partners could directly impact the company’s ability to maintain smooth and consistent operations.

To mitigate supply chain disruption risks, the Company prioritizes enhancing its supply chain programs and policies, focusing on sustainable and effective management practices. By implementing robust policies and processes, Hester aims to ensure business continuity and protect shareholder value through responsible supply chain management.

Investing in new technologies seeks to enhance the environmental and social impact of products and processes. Advancing vaccine development to address critical health challenges and severe diseases will lead to improved public health outcomes.

Advanced systems such as centralized chiller water supply and distribution exemplify sustainable water management and efficient resource utilization by enabling full water recycling, minimizing environmental impact, and enhancing operational efficiency.

The Company’s operations generate minimal recoverable waste, limited primarily to packaging materials such as thermocol, plastic wrapping, gel-packs, bottles and vials. These materials are routinely collected by end-customers and transferred to third-party recyclers for reuse or environmentally responsible disposal. No other specific waste streams arise from our processes that warrant internal reclamation.

Our internal Waste Management SOPs and Policy establishes clear guidelines for handling end-of-life electronic equipment and machinery. The Asset Team conducts periodic reviews to identify assets that have reached the end of their service life, and the Procurement Department then arranges for their transfer to certified waste dealers. Wherever feasible, components are salvaged for recycling or repurposing before any residual materials are sent for final disposal.

All hazardous and biological by-products of our manufacturing activities are managed under rigorously enforced protocols that comply fully with the State Pollution Control Board’s regulations. We engage only SPCB-approved waste-management partners to oversee the safe collection, treatment and final disposal of these materials. Detailed documentation is maintained at every stage to ensure complete traceability and regulatory adherence.

The entity has submitted its Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) application in relation to the plastic waste generation from its operations to the Pollution Control Board and is currently awaiting approval. It is actively liaising with the relevant authorities to ensure full compliance and will proceed as soon as further guidance is received.

Note to Q1 of Essential Indicator: The Company has reassessed the R&D and Capex incurred during the financial years 2024-25 and 2023-24 to specifically identify initiatives aimed at improving the environmental and social impact of its products. The total R&D and Capex incurred during each year has been considered as a base for the disclosures.   

Note to Q2 of Essential Indicator: The Company follows a structured procedure for sustainable sourcing. All new supply chain partners undergo mandatory evaluation on environmental, health & safety and sustainability parameters before onboarding. Existing partners are regularly assessed through quality checks and inspections. The Company ensures that each supplier holds an international or applicable domestic accreditation/certification, such as ISO.

Supplier selection incorporates WHO, GMP and cGMP standards within standard operating procedures and protocols. Rigorous measures are taken to maintain stringent quality standards, with periodic audits assessing product stability. Additionally, all suppliers must obtain environmental audit certifications from ISO and OHSAS.

The Company has established a standalone sustainable supply mechnisam and Supplier Code of Conduct, integrating sustainability parameters into its supply chain framework. Periodic ESG-based supplier assessments are conducted, and capacity-building workshops are organized for critical suppliers selected based on value, volume and dependency. To minimize the carbon footprint of logistics, the Company prioritises suppliers from nearby locations/districts. For waste management, only government-accredited suppliers are selected to ensure the highest compliance standards.

At Hester, we are dedicated to fostering an inclusive workplace and providing equal opportunities for all employees. Our Employee Manual and HR policies strictly prohibit discrimination based on race, caste, religion, color, ancestry, marital status, gender, sexual orientation, age, nationality, ethnic origin, disability, or any other characteristic protected by law.

The occupational health and safety  (OHS) management system comprehensively addresses all aspects of workplace safety, including hazard identification, risk assessment, emergency procedures, employee trainings, health surveillance and continuous safety performance monitoring.

The Company is an Integrated Management System (IMS) Certified organization, complying with ISO 9001:2015 (Quality Management System), ISO 14001:2015 (Environmental Management System), and ISO 45001:2018 (Occupational Health and Safety Management System). It takes proactive measures to prevent work-related injuries and illnesses, minimise risks and continuously enhance safety performance.

All has the Health & Safety (H&S) policy and framework is in place across all operations, ensuring workplace-wide coverage. The Company adheres and complies with the requirements to legal safety standards under various statutes, including the Factories Act, Indian Boilers Act, Environment Protection Act and The Epidemic Diseases Act.

The Company has implemented an on-site emergency plan and continues to strengthen safety protocols based on previous safety audits. This includes emergency preparedness drills and mock drills to ensure a swift and effective response to potential incidents.

The Company implemented the Hazard Identification and Risk Assessment (HIRA) process, where the Safety Manager, along with functional and technical teams, collaborates to identify hazards associated with company activities and evaluate risks against existing control measures. This process is conducted for both routine and non-routine activities to ensure comprehensive risk management.

On-site health & safety professionals regularly monitor workplace activities to ensure compliance with safety standards. Additionally, routine safety checks and maintenance are conducted to uphold the safety and reliability of all equipment and machinery.

The Company also emphasises continuous safety training and communication, ensuring that all employees remain informed about safety protocols, policies and initiatives. Regular safety messaging reinforces best practices and helps mitigate potential risks.

The entity prioritises the safety and well-being of employees through a structured Health and Safety Management System, implementing proactive measures to mitigate workplace risks. Risk assessment and hazard identification are conducted regularly through safety audits and inspections to address potential hazards before they pose a threat. Employees receive comprehensive health and safety training, covering safe work practices, proper use of PPE, and emergency response protocols for incidents such as fires and chemical spills.

To support overall well-being, the entity provides wellness and health programs, including regular health checks, ergonomic assessments, and mental health support. Workplace safety audits, both internal and external, ensure compliance with regulations, while accident reporting and investigation mechanisms help in preventing recurrence. Safety awareness is further reinforced through regular safety meetings, workplace signage, and annual events such as Safety Week 2025, where employees actively participate in safety training, drills, and wellness initiatives to foster a culture of workplace safety.

The Company remains committed to maintaining a safe and healthy work environment by addressing safety-related incidents promptly and proactively. A streamlined incident reporting and investigation system ensures that all safety concerns are thoroughly examined, with corrective actions taken to prevent recurrence. To enhance workplace protection, the entity has upgraded all necessary PPE to meet industry standards and ensure optimal safety for employees. Additionally, routine safety inspections and workplace safety training are conducted to identify and mitigate potential risks.

Employee preparedness is further strengthened through enhanced safety training programs, covering chemical handling, emergency procedures, and workplace safety best practices. Regular emergency drills and updated evacuation procedures ensure employees are well-equipped to respond to emergencies. Furthermore, recognizing the importance of mental well-being, the entity has introduced a mental health program that includes counseling services and stress management workshops.

To sustain ongoing improvements, regular safety committee meetings are held to review safety practices, discuss concerns, and implement necessary enhancements. These initiatives collectively reinforce a culture of safety and continuous improvement, ensuring a safer and more resilient workplace for all employees.

The Company uses email and other informal channels of communication as the internal mechanism for grievance redressal of human rights issues. The Company’s Human Rights Policy outlines the grievance redressal mechanism through the open channels of communication and as well as the mechanism to reach out to the Ombudsman in line with the Whistleblower Policy. The Ombudsman ensures the confidentiality of the complaints and grievances received. Any grievances in relation to human rights issues can be raised through email: human.resources@hester.in

The Company has a Whistle Blower Policy wherein all employees can report, without fear of retaliation, any wrong practices or unethical behaviour on discrimination or harassment which may have a detrimental effect on the organisation, including financial damage and impact on brand image.  

The details of the complainant are kept confidential and the authenticity of the complainant’s report is investigated by the assigned committee/ officer. The details regarding the investigation are also limited to the committee and kept confidential. The complainant is protected from any discrimination and harassment till the issue is resolved.
There were no significant risks/ concerns arising from the above assessments.

The Company adheres to Zero Liquid Discharge (ZLD) by treating, recycling and reusing all wastewater, ensuring no discharge into the environment. It employs advanced treatment methods such as Effluent Treatment, Reverse Osmosis, Multiple Effect Evaporation (MEE) and Agitated Thin Film Drying (ATFD) to enhance sustainability. This approach supports water conservation, regulatory compliance and environmental protection.

The Company is dedicated to reducing its carbon footprint through a series of strategic initiatives focused on both renewable energy adoption and energy efficiency. In a bid to shift away from fossil fuels, the Company has already installed a 100 KW rooftop solar power system on one of its buildings, with plans to expand solar energy capacity in the future. This shift to renewable energy is part of a broader effort to reduce dependence on non-renewable energy sources and decrease overall greenhouse gas emissions.

In addition to renewable energy initiatives, the Company has undertaken significant upgrades to improve energy efficiency across its operations. These include the implementation of a Central Compressed Air Distribution Network, the creation of a Central Chilled Water Distribution Network and eastablishment of ETP lab to moniter the water treatment continously. These improvements help optimize energy usage, reduce waste, water stewardship and contribute to lowering the overall carbon emissions generated by the company.

The Company also places a strong emphasis on long-term sustainability through tree plantation and afforestation drives. These annual initiatives, which are often tied to environmental awareness events like World Environment Day, aim to enhance carbon sequestration. By increasing green cover on-site, the Company is actively working to offset its emissions, contributing to both environmental health and its sustainability goals. These collective efforts demonstrate the Company's commitment to minimizing its environmental impact and fostering a greener future.

The Company has implemented an effective waste management system that includes segregation, recycling, proper disposal and hazardous waste treatment, ensuring compliance with environmental regulations. Sustainable initiatives, such as reducing chemical usage, adopting eco-friendly alternatives, and implementing waste reduction techniques, help mitigate environmental impact and promote long-term ecological responsibility.

A comprehensive waste management framework addresses various waste categories generated during operations. This includes biomedical waste from QC and R&D processes, ETP sludge from wastewater treatment, plastic waste from packaging materials and electronic waste from equipment disposal. All waste is managed through authorized third-party agencies for recycling, reuse, landfill, or incineration, in strict accordance with regulatory requirements.

To further enhance waste management efficiency, the Company is actively commissioning additional waste reduction initiatives and has established an in-house ETP testing laboratory for real-time monitoring of treated water, further strengthening efforts towards sustainability.

The Company has established a robust and systematic framework for receiving, addressing, and responding to consumer complaints and feedback. All complaints, whether received through customer service channels, emails, or direct submissions, are promptly logged and acknowledged, with each assigned a unique reference number for tracking. Complaints are reviewed and routed to the relevant department, such as Quality Assurance, Technical Services, or Regulatory Affairs, based on their nature and severity. Those lodged through consumer forums or regulatory bodies are escalated and handled in strict compliance with applicable laws and internal procedures.

For product-related complaints, particularly those involving quality concerns, the Quality Assurance department initiates a comprehensive investigation process. This includes a preliminary risk assessment, sample collection (if applicable), and technical evaluation. These steps run concurrently to ensure timely resolution. The complainant is kept informed throughout the process. Upon identifying the root cause, a corrective action plan is implemented, and a complaint summary report is generated. A final risk assessment is conducted before a formal response is shared with the complainant, resulting in the official closure of the complaint.

To proactively enhance customer satisfaction and product performance, the Company also conducts regular consumer satisfaction surveys and post-marketing surveillance. Insights gathered from these activities are analyzed for trends and used to improve product offerings, operational processes, and customer engagement strategies, ensuring continuous improvement and consumer trust.
